parentQuestion: The type of cartilage found in the external ear is ___.

scaffolds.q: What property does the external ear need to have?
options: Flexible but able to return to shape
scaffolds.explain: The external ear can be bent and folded, but it springs back to its original shape. It needs to be flexible yet maintain its form — perfect for funneling sound waves into the ear canal. Form follows function, darling.

scaffolds.q: Which protein fiber provides elasticity and ability to stretch and recoil?
scaffolds.explain: Elastin fibers provide elasticity — they can stretch and then recoil back to their original length. Collagen provides strength and resists stretching, but elastin is the recoil specialist. we'll see elastin working throughout the body.
